title: Requirements, ER Modeling, Functional Dependencies, and Normalization · Query Retrieval
summary: A growing incident system repeats team and member facts, mixes multiple values per cell, and produces update anomalies. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a normalized incident-system schema defense after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive entities, attributes, identifiers, relationships, optionality, and business rules from stakeholder language.
Model one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many relationships with minimum and maximum cardinality.
Identify functional dependencies and candidate keys from semantics rather than sample coincidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Lossless Dependency Design, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
